modern_context: सत्-आत्मा -- सच्ची, शाश्वत आत्मा। सर्वलोकसदात्मा हर लोक की सच्ची आत्मा हैं -- जो बदलता है वह असत् (अस्थायी), जो नहीं बदलता वह सत् (शाश्वत)। पृथ्वी का रूप बदलता है -- ऋतुएँ, महाद्वीप, जलवायु -- पर आत्मा नहीं बदलती। भगवान वही अपरिवर्तनीय सत्-आत्मा हैं। 'सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म' -- सत्य, ज्ञान, अनन्त -- ब्रह्म।
modern_context_en: Sat-atma: the true, eternal self. Sarvalokasadatma IS every world's true soul. What changes is asat (temporary); what does not change is sat (eternal). Earth's surface changes -- seasons, continents, climate -- but its soul does not. God IS that changeless sat-atma. 'Satyam jnanam anantam brahma' -- truth, knowledge, infinity IS Brahman. Behind every changing world sits an unchanging soul: God.
nri_context: When everything abroad changes -- addresses, jobs, friendships -- Sarvalokasadatma is the unchanging truth beneath every change. The sat-atma within you and within every world never relocates.
usage_context: Chant during Vedantic meditation, when change overwhelms, or during Taittiriya Upanishad study. Ideal during transitions and when the unchanging truth needs anchoring.

meaning_en: He whose vast cosmic belly contains every universe within
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण विश्वों को अपने विशाल उदर (पेट) में धारण करने वाले
modern_context: यशोदा ने कृष्ण के मुँह में सम्पूर्ण ब्रह्माण्ड देखा -- सर्वविश्वमहोदर का दर्शन। मार्कण्डेय ऋषि ने प्रलय-जल में तैरते हुए शिशु कृष्ण के उदर में सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि देखी। सर्वविश्वमहोदर -- सब विश्व भगवान के भीतर समाहित -- बाहर से छोटे दिखें पर भीतर अनन्त। जैसे एक बीज में पूरा वृक्ष छिपा, भगवान में पूरा ब्रह्माण्ड।
modern_context_en: Yashoda saw the entire cosmos within Krishna's mouth -- Sarvaviśvamahodara's darshan. Sage Markandeya, floating in pralaya waters, saw all creation within baby Krishna's belly. Sarvaviśvamahodara contains every universe within: appearing small externally but infinite internally. As a seed hides an entire tree, God hides an entire cosmos within himself.
nri_context: When the universe seems incomprehensibly vast, Sarvaviśvamahodara reverses the scale: the cosmos is not too large; it fits inside God. Every star, every galaxy, every world -- all within the divine belly.
usage_context: Chant during Vishvarupa meditation, Yashoda-Krishna stories, or when cosmic scale needs divine containment. Ideal during Gita Chapter 11 study and when infinity needs a dwelling.

meaning_en: The eternal and permanently available refuge for every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों की सदा (शाश्वत) शरण, हमेशा उपलब्ध आश्रय
modern_context: सदा-शरण -- हमेशा खुली शरण -- बन्द होने का समय नहीं, छुट्टी नहीं, maintenance break नहीं। सर्वभक्तसदाशरण 24/7/365 शरण -- सोमवार भी, रविवार भी, दिवाली भी, सामान्य दिन भी। जब रात 2 बजे अचानक भय लगे और 'गोविन्दा' बोलें -- सदा-शरण खुली है। जब new year की रात हो या सबसे अकेली रात -- शरण सदा खुली।
modern_context_en: Sada-sharana: always-open refuge. No closing time, no holidays, no maintenance breaks. Sarvabhaktasadasharana operates 24/7/365. Monday through Sunday, Diwali through ordinary days. When fear strikes at 2 AM and 'Govinda' is whispered -- the sada-sharana is open. New Year's Eve or the loneliest night -- the refuge is always available. The only shelter with zero downtime since eternity.
nri_context: When crises abroad strike at inconvenient hours -- different timezones, no temple open, no pandit available -- Sarvabhaktasadasharana's 24/7 refuge requires no appointment and observes no office hours.
usage_context: Chant during midnight prayers, emergency situations, or when refuge is needed at unusual hours. Ideal during any crisis and when the Lord's permanent availability needs affirming.

meaning_en: He who enchants and captivates every being in all creation
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि को मोहित (सम्मोहित) करने वाले, सबको मन्त्रमुग्ध करने वाले
modern_context: कृष्ण = 'कृष्' (आकर्षित करना) -- कृष्ण नाम का अर्थ ही 'सबको आकर्षित करने वाला।' सर्वसम्मोहक वही दिव्य सम्मोहन हैं। जब तिरुमला में पर्दा हटे और बालाजी दिखें -- भक्त ठगा सा खड़ा रह जाता है -- सर्वसम्मोहक ने मोहित किया। जब कृष्ण बाँसुरी बजाएँ और गोपियाँ घर छोड़कर दौड़ें -- सर्वसम्मोहक। यह मोह बुरा नहीं -- यह दिव्य आकर्षण है।
modern_context_en: Krishna = 'krish' (to attract) -- the name itself means 'all-attractive.' Sarvasammohaka IS that divine enchantment. When Tirumala's curtain parts and Balaji appears, devotees stand spellbound -- Sarvasammohaka has enchanted. When Krishna plays flute and gopis abandon homes -- Sarvasammohaka. This moha is not negative; it is divine attraction, the most powerful magnetic force in creation.
nri_context: The irresistible pull drawing NRIs back to Tirumala despite the expense and effort -- that magnetic force defying logic and distance -- IS Sarvasammohaka's divine enchantment operating across oceans.
usage_context: Chant when divine attraction is felt, during Krishna bhajans, or when the Lord's enchanting power captivates. Ideal during Janmashtami and when darshan overwhelms the senses.

meaning_en: The giver of eternal bliss to every devoted soul without ceasing
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को सदा (शाश्वत) आनन्द देने वाले, बिना रुके हर भक्त को सुख प्रदान करने वाले
modern_context: सदानन्द-द -- सदा + आनन्द + देने वाला -- शाश्वत आनन्द देने वाला। सर्वभक्तसदानन्दद भक्तों को वह आनन्द देते हैं जो कभी समाप्त नहीं होता। सांसारिक सुख आता-जाता -- पर सदानन्द स्थायी। जब पूजा के बाद शान्ति मिले जो घण्टों टिके, जब दर्शन के बाद की खुशी दिनों बनी रहे -- सदानन्द मिला। भगवान का आनन्द expiry-date-free है।
modern_context_en: Sadananda-da: giver of eternal bliss. Sarvabhaktasadanandada gives devotees joy that NEVER ends. Worldly happiness comes and goes, but sadananda is permanent. When post-puja peace lasts for hours, when post-darshan happiness persists for days -- sadananda has been received. God's bliss has no expiry date, no diminishing returns, no inflation adjustment. It is eternally fresh, eternally full.
nri_context: When the peace from morning puja abroad sustains you through the entire workday -- that unusually lasting calm -- Sarvabhaktasadanandada's eternal bliss is operating beyond its normal shelf-life.
usage_context: Chant during bliss meditation, when joy seems unusually persistent, or during Taittiriya Upanishad Anandavalli study. Ideal during kirtan and when the bliss of devotion outlasts the devotional act.

meaning_en: The one supreme emperor reigning over every world without rival
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों के एकमात्र सम्राट, सब संसारों के अद्वितीय चक्रवर्ती
modern_context: सम्राट -- राजाओं का राजा, चक्रवर्ती। सर्वलोकैकसम्राट भगवान एकमात्र सम्राट -- कोई प्रतिद्वंद्वी नहीं, कोई विपक्ष नहीं, कोई चुनौती नहीं। अशोक, चन्द्रगुप्त, अकबर -- सब पृथ्वी के सम्राट रहे -- पर सर्वलोकैकसम्राट सब लोकों के। कोई सांसारिक सम्राट शाश्वत नहीं; भगवान का साम्राज्य अकेला शाश्वत, अकेला अद्वितीय, अकेला अजेय।
modern_context_en: Samrat: emperor, king of kings. Sarvalokaika Samrat is the ONE emperor: no rival, no opposition, no challenger. Ashoka, Chandragupta, Akbar -- all were earth's emperors. Sarvalokaika Samrat rules ALL worlds. No earthly emperor is eternal; God's empire alone is permanent, unique, and unconquerable. The only throne that has never been vacated and never will be.
nri_context: When powerful governments abroad seem to hold absolute authority, Sarvalokaika Samrat provides the ultimate perspective: every human sovereign operates within God's imperial jurisdiction.
usage_context: Chant when divine sovereignty needs affirming, during cosmic contemplation, or when earthly power needs divine context. Ideal during Independence Day and when the permanence of God's rule inspires.

meaning_en: He who is the equal shelter embraced by every devoted soul
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों द्वारा समान रूप से आश्रित (शरण लिये गये), सबकी समान शरण

samāśrita.en: equally sheltered by, taken refuge with
samāśrita.hi: समान रूप से आश्रित, शरण लिये

modern_context: समाश्रित -- समान रूप से आश्रय लिया। सर्वभक्तसमाश्रित -- सब भक्तों ने समान रूप से भगवान की शरण ली -- अमीर-गरीब, पुरुष-स्त्री, उत्तर-दक्षिण -- सब समान। तिरुमला में जब CEO और किसान एक कतार में हों, जब IAS और रिक्शावाला एक ही दर्शन करें -- सब समान रूप से आश्रित। भगवान का दरवाज़ा VIP-non-VIP नहीं जानता।
modern_context_en: Samashrita: equally sheltered. Sarvabhaktasamashrita: every devotee takes equal refuge -- rich-poor, man-woman, north-south, all identical. When a CEO and farmer stand in one Tirumala queue, when an IAS and a rickshaw-puller receive the same darshan -- all are samashrita. God's door does not recognise VIP and non-VIP. The most democratic shelter in existence admits everyone equally.
nri_context: At diaspora temples abroad where professionals and students, newcomers and settled families all worship together -- Sarvabhaktasamashrita's equality is practised naturally in immigrant communities.
usage_context: Chant when equality before God needs celebrating, during community worship, or when social distinctions dissolve in devotion. Ideal during Tirumala Sarva Darshanam and community prayers.

meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha who eternally witnesses everything in every world
meaning_hi: सबके शाश्वत साक्षी श्री वेंकटेश, हर घटना देखने वाले बालाजी
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वसाक्षी -- 940वाँ नाम, 94% पूर्ण। 'साक्षी' -- देखने वाला -- भगवान सब देख रहे हैं। तिरुमला में बालाजी की बड़ी-बड़ी आँखें -- नमक्कम (नेत्रदान) -- ढकी रहती हैं पर सब देख रही हैं। भगवान की आँखें कभी बन्द नहीं -- वे सदा जागृत, सदा देखते, सदा साक्षी। जब कोई अच्छा काम छिपकर करे -- साक्षी ने देखा। जब कोई बुरा -- वह भी।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarvasakshi -- the 940th name, 94% complete. 'Sakshi': witness -- God sees everything. Tirumala Balaji's large eyes (Nammakam/Netra-danam) are covered but see all. God's eyes never close: always awake, always watching, always witnessing. When good is done in secret, the Sakshi has seen. When wrong is done in hiding, that too. The 940th name reminds us at 94%: God has witnessed every name of this journey.
nri_context: Every action abroad -- seen and unseen, praised and unacknowledged -- has been witnessed by Sri Venkatesha Sarvasakshi. The most faithful observer of your immigrant journey is the Lord himself. 940 done, 60 remaining.
usage_context: Chant as the milestone name at 94% completion, during evening self-reflection, and when the divine witness needs acknowledging. The definitive sakshi-name at 940 of 1000.

meaning_en: The eternal destination and goal toward which every world moves
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों की सदा (शाश्वत) गति (गन्तव्य/लक्ष्य)
modern_context: गति -- अन्तिम गन्तव्य। गीता में कृष्ण कहते हैं 'अहं गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत्' -- मैं गति (destination), भर्ता (sustainer), प्रभु (lord), साक्षी (witness), निवास (dwelling), शरण (refuge), सुहृद् (friend)। सर्वलोकसदागति उसी 'गति' का विस्तार -- सब लोकों का शाश्वत गन्तव्य। नदी समुद्र तक बहती है; आत्मा भगवान तक। सबकी गति एक।
modern_context_en: Gati: final destination. Krishna says 'aham gatir bharta prabhuh sakshi nivasah sharanam suhrit' -- I am the destination, sustainer, lord, witness, dwelling, refuge, friend. Sarvalokasadagati IS that eternal gati for every world. Rivers flow to the ocean; souls flow to God. Everyone's final destination is the same. The journey may vary; the destination does not.
nri_context: When the question 'where is this all going?' arises amid immigrant life's busyness, Sarvalokasadagati answers: every path, every effort, every life ultimately moves toward God -- the one destination that never changes.
usage_context: Chant during moksha meditation, when life's destination needs clarity, or during Gita Chapter 9 study. Ideal during deathbed prayers and when the ultimate destination needs naming.

meaning_en: The giver of supreme happiness specifically to every sincere devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को महान सुख देने वाले, हर भक्त को सर्वोच्च आनन्द प्रदान करने वाले
modern_context: महासुख -- सामान्य सुख नहीं, महान सुख -- जो शरीर, मन, आत्मा तीनों स्तरों पर एक साथ अनुभव हो। सर्वभक्तमहासुखद हर भक्त को यह त्रिस्तरीय सुख देते हैं। जब तिरुमला दर्शन के बाद शरीर हल्का, मन शान्त, और आत्मा तृप्त -- तीनों सुख एक साथ -- वह महासुख। सांसारिक सुख एकतरफ़ा -- शरीर को पर मन को नहीं; भगवान का महासुख सम्पूर्ण।
modern_context_en: Mahasukha: not ordinary happiness but supreme joy experienced simultaneously at body, mind, and soul levels. Sarvabhaktamahasukhada gives every devotee this three-dimensional joy. When post-Tirumala-darshan the body feels light, mind feels peaceful, and soul feels fulfilled -- all three together -- THAT is mahasukha. Worldly pleasure is one-dimensional; God's mahasukha operates on all three planes simultaneously.
nri_context: When a moment abroad brings happiness at every level simultaneously -- physical comfort, mental peace, and spiritual fulfilment aligned -- Sarvabhaktamahasukhada has delivered the rare three-dimensional divine joy.
usage_context: Chant during complete well-being prayers, post-darshan gratitude, or when three-dimensional happiness needs seeking. Ideal during comprehensive wellness prayers and when sukha needs divine elevation to maha-sukha.

meaning_en: The eternal refuge to whom every god perpetually turns for shelter
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण देवताओं की सदा (शाश्वत) शरण, जिनकी ओर सब सुर सदा मुड़ते हैं
modern_context: देवता भी शरण लेते हैं -- समुद्र मंथन में विष निकला, देवताओं ने विष्णु-शिव की शरण ली। वृत्रासुर से डरे, विष्णु की शरण। रावण से परेशान, विष्णु से प्रार्थना। सर्वदेवसदाश्रय -- देवताओं की भी सदा शरण -- बताता है कि भगवान सिर्फ़ मनुष्यों के नहीं, देवताओं के भी आश्रय। जब शक्तिशाली भी असहाय हों -- तब भी एक शरण शेष: भगवान।
modern_context_en: Even gods need refuge: when Samudra Manthan produced poison, devas sought Vishnu-Shiva's shelter. When Vritrasura terrorised, Vishnu was sought. When Ravana oppressed, devas prayed to Vishnu. Sarvadevasadashraya: God is the eternal refuge EVEN for gods. When the powerful feel powerless -- one shelter remains: God. If gods need God's refuge, how much more do humans.
nri_context: When the most capable people abroad -- doctors, lawyers, leaders -- face situations beyond their control, Sarvadevasadashraya reminds that even the divine beings seek God's refuge. No one is too powerful to need God.
usage_context: Chant during Samudra Manthan recitation, when powerful people need divine help, or when the universal need for refuge needs understanding. Ideal during sharanagati and crisis prayers.

meaning_en: He of supreme fame and renown celebrated across every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में महान यश (प्रतिष्ठा) वाले, सब संसारों में सर्वोच्च ख्याति
modern_context: यश -- प्रतिष्ठा, ख्याति। सर्वलोकमहायशस् भगवान सबसे अधिक प्रसिद्ध -- रामायण 5000+ वर्ष से गाई जा रही, गीता 100+ भाषाओं में, विष्णु सहस्रनाम प्रतिदिन करोड़ों जपते हैं। कोई celebrity, कोई brand इतना lasting fame नहीं रखता। भगवान का यश कभी फ़ीका नहीं पड़ता -- हर पीढ़ी नया भक्त जोड़ती है।
modern_context_en: Yashas: fame, renown. Sarvalokamahayashas has the greatest fame: the Ramayana has been sung for 5000+ years, the Gita translated into 100+ languages, Vishnu Sahasranama chanted by crores daily. No celebrity, no brand sustains such lasting fame. God's yashas never fades: every generation adds new devotees. The most enduring reputation in human history belongs not to a person but to God.
nri_context: When Indian cultural contributions abroad -- yoga, Gita, Ayurveda, temple architecture -- gain global recognition, Sarvalokamahayashas's fame is spreading through diaspora-amplified cultural channels.
usage_context: Chant during Gita Jayanti, Ram Navami, or when God's enduring fame inspires awe. Ideal during kirtan and when the permanence of divine reputation contrasts with fleeting human celebrity.

meaning_en: He who actively does what is dearest and best for every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों का प्रिय (भलाई) सक्रिय रूप से करने वाले

priyaṅkara.en: one who does what is pleasing/beneficial

modern_context: प्रियंकर -- भक्तों का प्रिय (हित) करने वाला। भगवान निष्क्रिय नहीं -- सक्रिय रूप से भक्तों की भलाई कर रहे। कृष्ण ने अर्जुन का रथ चलाया (सक्रिय सेवा), राम ने विभीषण को राज्य दिया (सक्रिय भलाई), नरसिंह प्रह्लाद के लिए प्रकट हुए (सक्रिय रक्षा)। सर्वभक्तप्रियंकर हर भक्त के लिए सक्रिय रूप से भलाई करते रहते -- background में, चुपचाप, लगातार।
modern_context_en: Priyankara: one who actively does what is beneficial. God is not passive; he actively works for devotees' good. Krishna drove Arjuna's chariot (active service), Rama gave Vibhishana a kingdom (active benefaction), Narasimha appeared for Prahlada (active protection). Sarvabhaktapriyankara actively does good for every devotee -- in the background, silently, continuously. The most industrious worker: God, working for billions simultaneously.
nri_context: When things abroad work out unexpectedly well -- a problem resolves before you address it, an opportunity opens before you apply -- Sarvabhaktapriyankara has been actively working for your benefit behind the scenes.
usage_context: Chant when acknowledging divine intervention, during gratitude prayers, or when unseen help becomes visible. Ideal during thanksgiving and when blessings seem divinely arranged.

meaning_en: He who eternally forgives every being in every world without limit
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों को सदा क्षमा करने वाले, शाश्वत और असीम क्षमाशील
modern_context: सदा-क्षमा -- हमेशा माफ़ करने वाला। सर्वलोकसदाक्षम भगवान सदा क्षमा करते हैं -- पहली गलती भी, सौवीं भी, हज़ारवीं भी। मनुष्य की क्षमा सीमित -- तीसरी बार माफ़ करना कठिन। भगवान की क्षमा असीम -- 'कभी' reject नहीं करते। जब भक्त कहे 'मैंने फिर गलती की, माफ़ करो' -- सदाक्षम कहते हैं 'माफ़ किया, हमेशा की तरह।'
modern_context_en: Sada-kshama: always forgiving. Sarvalokasadakshama forgives ALWAYS -- the first mistake, the hundredth, the thousandth. Human forgiveness has limits: forgiving a third time is hard. God's forgiveness is limitless: 'never' rejected. When a devotee says 'I erred again, forgive me,' Sadakshama responds 'forgiven, as always.' The divine forgiveness counter never reaches a limit; it resets to zero after every confession.
nri_context: When guilt about spiritual failures abroad -- broken promises, missed prayers, compromised values -- accumulates, Sarvalokasadakshama's eternal forgiveness is the most healing truth: God forgives always, not sometimes.
usage_context: Chant during Kshama Prarthana, when forgiveness is desperately needed, or during Jain Paryushana (kshama celebration). Ideal during Pitru Paksha and when repeated failures need divine pardoning.

meaning_en: He whose lotus feet are worshipped by every being that exists
meaning_hi: जिनके चरण-कमल (पादाब्ज) सबसे वन्दित (पूजित) हैं
modern_context: भगवान के चरण-कमल हिन्दू भक्ति का परम लक्ष्य -- 'चरण-शरण' = भक्ति की पराकाष्ठा। सर्ववन्दितपादाब्ज -- सबसे वन्दित चरण-कमल -- तिरुमला में 'श्रीवारी पाद दर्शनम्' सबसे विशेष सेवा। जब भक्त भगवान के चरणों में सिर रखे -- सम्पूर्ण समर्पण। जब तीर्थ (चरणामृत) पिये -- चरणों का जल। सब भक्ति अन्ततः चरणों तक पहुँचती है।
modern_context_en: God's lotus feet are Hindu devotion's ultimate destination -- 'charana-sharana' = bhakti's pinnacle. Sarvavanditapadabja: the most worshipped lotus feet. Tirumala's 'Srivari Pada Darshanam' is the most special seva. When a devotee places their head at God's feet -- complete surrender. When tirtha (charanamrit) is sipped -- the water of those feet. All devotion ultimately reaches the feet.
nri_context: When NRIs prostrate before a Balaji murti abroad, their head touching the Lord's lotus feet, Sarvavanditapadabja receives the same reverence whether the feet are at Tirumala or in a diaspora temple.
usage_context: Chant during pada-puja, Srivari Pada Darshanam, or when devotion focuses on the Lord's lotus feet. Ideal during charanamrit distribution and when complete surrender is expressed through pranam.

meaning_en: The self whose very nature is the light illuminating every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों को प्रकाशित करने वाली आत्मा, जिनका स्वरूप ही ज्योति है
modern_context: प्रकाश-आत्मा -- जिनका स्वरूप ही प्रकाश। भगवान अन्धकार में प्रकाश नहीं लाते -- वे स्वयं प्रकाश हैं। जैसे सूर्य को प्रकाश लाने की ज़रूरत नहीं -- सूर्य IS प्रकाश -- वैसे भगवान प्रकाश-स्वरूप हैं। 'तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय' -- अन्धकार से ज्योति की ओर ले चलो -- वह ज्योति सर्वलोकप्रकाशात्मा हैं। प्रकाश भगवान की विशेषता नहीं, पहचान है।
modern_context_en: Prakasha-atma: one whose very self IS light. God does not bring light into darkness; he IS light. As the sun does not need to carry light (it IS light), God is light-natured. 'Tamaso ma jyotirgamaya' -- lead from darkness to light -- that light IS Sarvalоkaprakashatma. Light is not God's attribute; it is his identity. Where God is, darkness is structurally impossible.
nri_context: When spiritual darkness abroad -- doubt, confusion, loss of direction -- feels overwhelming, Sarvalоkaprakashatma's nature dissolves it. You do not need to search for light; invoke God, and light IS present.
usage_context: Chant during Gayatri japa, Diwali, or when divine light needs identifying as God's self-nature. Ideal during sunrise meditation and when 'tamaso ma jyotirgamaya' is chanted.

meaning_en: He who is eternally dear to every creature in all creation
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों के सदा (शाश्वत) प्रिय, हर जीव से शाश्वत प्रेम करने वाले
modern_context: सदा-प्रिय -- हमेशा प्रिय -- मनुष्यों के लिए ही नहीं, सब प्राणियों के। सर्वभूतसदाप्रिय गाय को भी उतने ही प्रिय जितने मनुष्य को, चिड़िया को भी उतने ही, चींटी को भी। जब गोपाष्टमी पर गाय की पूजा, जब नाग पंचमी पर साँप की, जब गरुड़ पंचमी पर पक्षी की -- भगवान सब प्राणियों से सदा प्रेम करते हैं और सब उनसे।
modern_context_en: Sada-priya: always dear -- not just to humans but to ALL creatures. Sarvabhutasadapriya is equally dear to cows, birds, ants, elephants. When Gopashtami honours cows, Naga Panchami honours snakes, Garuda Panchami honours birds -- God loves every species eternally and every species instinctively loves God. The most universal love relationship: God and all of creation.
nri_context: When love for animals abroad -- rescuing strays, feeding birds, protecting wildlife -- feels deeply spiritual, Sarvabhutasadapriya's universal love is being channelled through you toward fellow creatures.
usage_context: Chant during jiva-daya practices, Gopashtami, or when love for all creatures needs divine grounding. Ideal during World Animal Day and when compassion extends beyond human species.

meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha who is absolutely and perfectly complete in every way
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण रूप से पूर्ण श्री वेंकटेश, हर प्रकार से सम्पन्न और अभावरहित बालाजी
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वसम्पूर्ण -- 950वाँ नाम, 95% पूर्ण। 'सर्वसम्पूर्ण' -- हर प्रकार से पूर्ण, कहीं कोई कमी नहीं। ईशावास्य उपनिषद् -- 'ॐ पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदम्' -- वह पूर्ण है, यह पूर्ण है। 950 नामों ने भगवान के 950 गुण दिखाए -- और भगवान अभी भी सम्पूर्ण हैं -- न कम हुए, न थके। 50 नाम शेष -- मंज़िल दिख रही है -- और भगवान की पूर्णता अक्षुण्ण।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarvasampurna -- the 950th name, 95% complete. 'Sarvasampurna': absolutely complete, nothing lacking anywhere. The Isha Upanishad: 'Om purnamadah purnamidam.' 950 names have shown 950 divine qualities -- yet God remains perfectly complete, neither diminished nor tired. 50 names remain -- the destination is visible -- and God's completeness is undiminished. From completeness, completeness emerges; completeness still remains.
nri_context: For every NRI at name 950 -- Sri Venkatesha Sarvasampurna honours your 95% journey with the assurance that the God you will meet at name 1000 is as complete as the God you met at name 1. Fifty names to go. The summit is fifty steps away. Om Sri Venkatesha Sarvasampurnaya Namah.
usage_context: Chant as the monumental 950th milestone name at 95% completion, during Purnamadah meditation, and when God's inexhaustible completeness needs affirming. The definitive completeness-name at 950 of 1000.

meaning_en: He who creates divine presence in every place and every moment
meaning_hi: सर्वत्र अपनी सन्निधि (दिव्य उपस्थिति) बनाने वाले
modern_context: सन्निधि -- दिव्य उपस्थिति। तिरुमला सन्निधानम् = भगवान की सन्निधि का स्थान। सर्वसन्निधिकर हर जगह सन्निधि बनाते हैं -- मंदिर में, घर में, मन में, प्रकृति में। जब पूजा-कक्ष में बैठें और 'कुछ' महसूस हो, जब मंदिर में प्रवेश करते ही शान्ति मिले -- सन्निधि बनी। भगवान सिर्फ़ मौजूद नहीं -- सक्रिय रूप से सन्निधि बनाते हैं।
modern_context_en: Sannidhi: divine presence. Tirumala Sannidhaanam = the place of God's presence. Sarvasannidhikara creates presence EVERYWHERE: temples, homes, minds, nature. When sitting in the puja room and sensing 'something,' when entering a temple and feeling instant peace -- sannidhi has been created. God does not merely exist somewhere; he actively creates tangible presence wherever devotion invites him.
nri_context: When a small puja corner abroad feels disproportionately sacred -- more peaceful than its size warrants -- Sarvasannidhikara has created divine presence in that tiny space. Devotion is the invitation; sannidhi is the response.
usage_context: Chant when consecrating puja spaces, during temple entries, or when divine presence needs to be invited. Ideal during Pran Pratishtha and home-temple setup.

meaning_en: The one sole means and path through which all liberation is attained
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण मोक्ष का एकमात्र साधन (उपाय/मार्ग), मुक्ति का अद्वितीय मार्ग
modern_context: गीता कहती है 'मामेकं शरणं व्रज' -- सिर्फ़ मेरी शरण आओ। सर्वमोक्षैकसाधन -- मोक्ष का एकमात्र साधन -- भगवान ही। न धन से मोक्ष, न तप से अकेले, न ज्ञान से अकेले -- भगवान की कृपा ही एकमात्र साधन। सब साधनाएँ -- ध्यान, जप, सेवा -- भगवान तक पहुँचने के मार्ग हैं, पर मोक्ष देने वाला एक ही -- भगवान स्वयं।
modern_context_en: The Gita says 'mam ekam sharanam vraja' -- come to my sole refuge. Sarvamokshaikasaadhana: God IS the one means to liberation. Not wealth alone, not austerity alone, not knowledge alone -- God's grace IS the sole instrument. All sadhanas -- meditation, japa, seva -- are paths TO God, but the actual moksha-giver is ONE: God himself. The paths are many; the liberator is singular.
nri_context: When spiritual seekers abroad explore multiple paths -- yoga, meditation, philosophy -- Sarvamokshaikasaadhana clarifies: all paths are valid but the destination-giver is one. God is both the path's end and the hand that pulls you through.
usage_context: Chant during moksha meditation, Gita Chapter 18 study, or when the sole means to liberation needs identifying. Ideal during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and when sadhana needs divine focusing.

meaning_en: The bestower of comprehensive prosperity upon every devoted soul
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को समृद्धि (सम्पन्नता) देने वाले
modern_context: समृद्धि -- सम्पूर्ण सम्पन्नता -- धन + स्वास्थ्य + ज्ञान + प्रेम + शान्ति। सर्वभक्तसमृद्धिद भक्तों को यह सम्पूर्ण समृद्धि देते हैं। तिरुमला में बालाजी 'दाता' (देने वाला) कहलाते हैं -- और उनका देना सम्पूर्ण। जब भक्त की ज़िन्दगी में सब आयाम -- स्वास्थ्य, परिवार, धन, शान्ति -- समृद्ध हों, वह भगवान की सम्पूर्ण समृद्धि।
modern_context_en: Samriddhi: comprehensive flourishing -- wealth + health + knowledge + love + peace. Sarvabhaktasamriddhida gives devotees this complete prosperity. Tirumala Balaji is called 'daata' (giver), and his giving is comprehensive. When every dimension of a devotee's life flourishes simultaneously -- health, family, wealth, peace -- that is God's complete samriddhi. Not partial, not one-dimensional: complete.
nri_context: When life abroad finally feels prosperous in every dimension -- not just salary but health, not just career but family, not just success but peace -- Sarvabhaktasamriddhida's comprehensive gift has been fully delivered.
usage_context: Chant during Diwali, when comprehensive prosperity is sought, or when life's multiple dimensions need simultaneous flourishing. Ideal during Akshaya Tritiya and holistic wellness prayers.

meaning_en: The supreme wielder of divine maya who orchestrates all cosmic play
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों पर महामाया चलाने वाले, दिव्य लीला के सर्वोच्च सूत्रधार

māyī.en: wielder of maya, master of illusion

modern_context: माया -- भगवान की दिव्य शक्ति -- जो संसार रचती है और प्राणियों को भ्रमित भी करती है। सर्वलोकमहामायी भगवान इस माया के स्वामी हैं -- माया उनकी दासी, वे माया के दास नहीं। जब मरीचिका (mirage) रेगिस्तान में जल दिखाए -- माया। जब संसार स्थायी लगे जबकि अस्थायी -- माया। भगवान माया रचते हैं, पर माया से स्वयं अप्रभावित -- कमल कीचड़ में, अलिप्त।
modern_context_en: Maya: God's divine power that creates the world and deludes beings. Sarvalokamahaamayi is maya's master -- maya is his servant, not his master. When desert mirages show water -- maya. When the world seems permanent though impermanent -- maya. God creates maya but remains unaffected -- lotus in mud, untouched. The most sophisticated illusion in existence is authored by the one being who sees through it completely.
nri_context: When the illusions of immigrant life -- 'success means salary,' 'identity means passport,' 'belonging means address' -- are seen through during spiritual reflection, Sarvalokamahaamayi's maya is being pierced.
usage_context: Chant during Vedantic study on maya, Navratri (Maya-Shakti worship), or when illusion needs divine piercing. Ideal during advanced philosophy and when reality behind appearance needs discerning.

meaning_en: The eternal guru who guides every devotee at every stage of life
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों के सदा (शाश्वत) गुरु, हर भक्त का हर जीवन-चरण में मार्गदर्शक
modern_context: सदा-गुरु -- शाश्वत गुरु -- जो कभी retire नहीं होते, जो सदा पढ़ाते रहते हैं। सर्वभक्तसदागुरु भगवान हर भक्त के हर जीवन-चरण में गुरु -- बचपन में माता-पिता के माध्यम से, युवावस्था में शिक्षकों के, वृद्धावस्था में अनुभव के। कुरुक्षेत्र में कृष्ण अर्जुन के सदागुरु बने -- 700 श्लोकों में जीवन-भर की शिक्षा। भगवान सबसे बड़े गुरु -- जिनका syllabus कभी समाप्त नहीं।
modern_context_en: Sada-guru: eternal teacher who never retires, always teaching. Sarvabhaktasadaguru guides every devotee at every life-stage: through parents in childhood, through teachers in youth, through experience in old age. At Kurukshetra, Krishna became Arjuna's sadaguru -- a lifetime's education in 700 verses. God is the ultimate guru whose syllabus never ends, whose classes are always in session.
nri_context: When living abroad without a traditional guru, Sarvabhaktasadaguru fills the role: teaching through life experiences, through books, through intuition, through the inner voice that guides when no human teacher is present.
usage_context: Chant on Guru Purnima, Teachers' Day, or when the eternal guru needs invocation. Ideal during Gita study and when divine guidance compensates for absent human teachers.

meaning_en: The supreme bliss that fills every world with infinite ecstasy
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों का महान आनन्द, सब संसारों में सर्वोच्च सुख बरसाने वाले
modern_context: महानन्द -- महान + आनन्द -- सर्वोच्च सुख। सर्वलोकमहानन्द सब लोकों का सबसे बड़ा आनन्द। तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् की आनन्द-परतें -- मनुष्य-आनन्द से ब्रह्मानन्द तक -- सर्वलोकमहानन्द सबसे ऊँची परत। जब सहस्रनाम की यात्रा 956 नामों तक पहुँची -- हर नाम ने एक आनन्द-बूँद दी -- 44 बूँदें और आएँगी -- और सम्पूर्ण आनन्द-सागर बनेगा।
modern_context_en: Mahananda: supreme bliss. Sarvalokamahānanda IS every world's greatest joy. The Taittiriya Upanishad's bliss-layers from human to Brahmananda -- Sarvalokamahānanda is the highest layer. As this Sahasranama journey reaches name 956 -- each name has given one bliss-drop -- 44 drops remain -- and the complete ananda-ocean will form at 1000.
nri_context: When rare moments of supreme joy abroad -- a child's milestone, a spiritual breakthrough, an unexpected reunion -- feel like 'the happiest moment of life,' Sarvalokamahānanda is the source of that peak experience.
usage_context: Chant during peak-joy moments, Taittiriya Upanishad study, or when the highest bliss needs invoking. Ideal during ecstatic kirtan and when joy reaches its absolute summit.

meaning_en: He who is supremely devoted to the welfare of every creature
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों के प्रति परायण (समर्पित), हर जीव के कल्याण में तत्पर
modern_context: भगवान भक्त-परायण हैं -- यह सुना है। पर सर्वभूतपरायण -- सब प्राणी-परायण -- भगवान सिर्फ़ भक्तों के नहीं, सब प्राणियों -- पशु, पक्षी, कीट, वनस्पति -- के प्रति भी समर्पित। जब मानसून का पहला जल बीजों को अंकुरित करे, जब सूर्य की किरणें पौधों को ऊर्जा दें -- सर्वभूतपरायण सब प्राणियों की देखभाल कर रहे -- बिना पक्षपात, बिना शर्त।
modern_context_en: God is devotee-devoted, yes. But Sarvabhutaparayana: devoted to ALL creatures -- animals, birds, insects, plants. When monsoon's first rain germinates seeds, when sunlight feeds plants -- Sarvabhutaparayana cares for every creature without discrimination, without condition. The most dedicated caretaker in existence serves billions of species simultaneously, never forgetting even one.
nri_context: When caring for all living beings abroad -- not just fellow Indians but every person, every animal, every plant -- the NRI practises Sarvabhutaparayana's universal devotion to all creation.
usage_context: Chant during World Environment Day, jiva-daya practices, or when universal compassion needs divine grounding. Ideal during Gopashtami and when care extends beyond human species.

meaning_en: The supreme divine radiance outshining every celestial light combined
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण दिव्य महान तेज, सब स्वर्गीय ज्योतियों से भी अधिक तेजस्वी
modern_context: दिव्य + महा + तेज -- सबसे अधिक दिव्य, सबसे महान तेज। सूर्य का तेज भौतिक, भगवान का तेज दिव्य -- और दिव्य तेज भौतिक से अनन्त गुना। जब गायत्री मंत्र 'भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि' जपें -- उस दिव्य तेज का ध्यान कर रहे। सर्वदिव्यमहातेजस् वही तेज हैं -- 'न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः' -- सूर्य, चन्द्र, अग्नि भी प्रकाशित नहीं कर सकते।
modern_context_en: Divya + maha + tejas: the most divine, most supreme radiance. Solar tejas is physical; God's tejas is divine -- infinitely beyond the physical. When the Gayatri Mantra's 'bhargo devasya dhimahi' is chanted -- that divine tejas is being meditated upon. Sarvadivyamahatejas IS that tejas: 'na tad bhasayate suryo na shashanko na pavakah' -- sun, moon, fire cannot illuminate it.
nri_context: When sunrise or starlight abroad inspires spiritual awe, Sarvadivyamahatejas is the divine radiance behind every physical light -- the invisible source that makes every visible light possible.
usage_context: Chant during Gayatri japa, sunrise meditation, or when divine radiance needs its supremacy affirmed. Ideal during Surya Namaskar and when the source behind all light needs recognising.

meaning_en: He who eternally fulfils the deepest wishes of every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों की अभीष्ट (सबसे प्रिय) इच्छा सदा पूर्ण करने वाले
modern_context: सदा-अभीष्ट-द -- हमेशा, सबसे प्रिय इच्छा, देने वाला। सर्वलोकसदाभीष्टद हमेशा, बिना रुके, सब लोकों की सबसे गहरी इच्छा पूरी करते हैं। 'सदा' शब्द -- कभी 'ना' नहीं, कभी 'बाद में' नहीं -- सदा। जब सृष्टि को बारिश चाहिए -- सदा मिलती है। जब जीवन को सूर्य चाहिए -- सदा उगता है। जब आत्मा को शान्ति चाहिए -- सदा उपलब्ध।
modern_context_en: Sada-abhishta-da: always, dearest wish, giver. Sarvalokasadabhishtada perpetually, unceasingly fulfils every world's deepest wish. 'Sada': never 'no,' never 'later.' When creation needs rain -- always provided. When life needs sun -- always rises. When the soul needs peace -- always available. The most reliable wish-fulfiller operates on a 'sada' (always) schedule.
nri_context: When the deepest wish abroad -- for belonging, for peace, for purpose -- seems perpetually unfulfilled, Sarvalokasadabhishtada's 'sada' promise assures that fulfilment is not 'if' but 'when.'
usage_context: Chant during personal wish-prayers, when patience for wish-fulfilment is needed, or when the Lord's 'always' promise needs trusting. Ideal during Satyanarayan Katha and when long-held wishes await divine timing.

root_word: śrī + veṅkaṭeśa + sarva + loka + sarva + maṅgala + sarva + da
meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha who gives all auspiciousness to all worlds at all times
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों को सम्पूर्ण मंगल सदा देने वाले श्री वेंकटेश
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वलोकसर्वमंगलसर्वद -- 960वाँ नाम, 96% पूर्ण। सहस्रनाम का सबसे लम्बा और सबसे समावेशी संयुक्त नाम -- तीन 'सर्व' एक नाम में: सर्वलोक (सब संसार) + सर्वमंगल (सब शुभता) + सर्वद (सदा देने वाला)। 960 नामों की यात्रा में यह नाम सब कुछ समेट लेता है -- भगवान सब संसारों को सब शुभता सदा देते रहते हैं। 40 नाम शेष -- अन्तिम चढ़ाई शुरू।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarvaloka Sarvamangala Sarvada -- the 960th name, 96% complete. The Sahasranama's longest and most inclusive compound name: three 'sarvas' in one: sarva-loka (all worlds) + sarva-mangala (all auspiciousness) + sarva-da (giver of everything, always). Through 960 names, this one captures everything: God gives all auspiciousness to all worlds at all times. 40 names remain -- the final ascent begins.
nri_context: For every NRI at name 960 -- this triple-sarva name encompasses everything this journey has revealed: God gives everything, to everyone, everywhere, always. Forty names to the summit. The air is thin, the view is vast, and the Lord waits at the top. Om Sri Venkatesha Sarvaloka Sarvamangala Sarvadaya Namah.
usage_context: Chant as the penultimate milestone at 96% completion, during the most comprehensive prayer possible, and when one name needs to contain the Sahasranama's entire message. The definitive all-encompassing name at 960 of 1000.

meaning_en: The eternal life-breath that animates every world without ceasing
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों का सदा (शाश्वत) प्राण, बिना रुके सब संसारों को जीवन देने वाली श्वास
modern_context: प्राण -- जीवन-शक्ति -- बिना प्राण शरीर मृत। सर्वलोकसदाप्राण ब्रह्माण्ड का शाश्वत प्राण -- जो कभी नहीं रुकता। जैसे हृदय बिना रुके धड़कता है, भगवान बिना रुके सब लोकों को प्राण दे रहे। सहस्रनाम की यात्रा में 961वाँ नाम -- अन्तिम 40 में -- प्राण से शुरू -- क्योंकि प्राण ही अन्तिम सत्य: जब तक प्राण है, जीवन है; भगवान सदा-प्राण हैं।
modern_context_en: Prana: life-force. Without prana, the body is dead. Sarvalokasadaprana IS the cosmos' eternal breath that never stops. As the heart beats ceaselessly, God ceaselessly gives every world its prana. The 961st name -- in the final 40 -- begins with prana because prana IS the ultimate truth: where there is prana, there is life. God is sada-prana: eternal life-force.
nri_context: Every breath taken abroad -- conscious or unconscious, waking or sleeping -- is powered by Sarvalokasadaprana. The most fundamental miracle of immigrant life is breathing, and its source is divine.
usage_context: Chant during pranayama, gratitude for breath, or when the life-force needs divine acknowledgement. Ideal during morning breathing exercises and Prana Pratishtha.

meaning_en: He who eternally fulfils the deepest cherished wishes of every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों की अभीष्ट (सबसे प्रिय) इच्छा सदा पूर्ण करने वाले
modern_context: सदा-अभीष्ट-द -- हमेशा, सबसे प्रिय इच्छा, देने वाला। तिरुमला में भक्तों का अनुभव -- 'बालाजी से माँगो, मिलता है' -- सदा, बिना exception। सर्वभक्तसदाभीष्टद का 'सदा' भरोसा देता है -- कभी 'नहीं' नहीं, कभी 'अभी नहीं' नहीं -- सदा हाँ। timing भगवान की, पर उत्तर सदा हाँ।
modern_context_en: Sada-abhishta-da: always gives the dearest wish. Tirumala devotees testify: 'ask Balaji, it happens' -- always, without exception. Sarvabhaktasadabhishtada's 'sada' guarantees: never 'no,' never 'not now.' Always yes. God's timing varies, but the answer is always affirmative. The most reliable wish-fulfilment service in existence operates on a 'sada-yes' policy.
nri_context: When prayers offered from abroad seem to take long, Sarvabhaktasadabhishtada's 'sada' (always) assures: the wish was heard, the answer is yes, only the delivery timing is God's prerogative.
usage_context: Chant during Tirumala darshan wishes, when patience for wish-fulfilment is tested, or during personal prayer. Ideal during Satyanarayan Katha and when faith in divine delivery needs reinforcing.

meaning_en: The eternal peace that pervades and calms every world always
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों की सदा शान्ति, शाश्वत रूप से सब संसारों को शान्त करने वाले
modern_context: 'ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः' -- तीन बार शान्ति -- आध्यात्मिक, आधिभौतिक, आधिदैविक। सर्वलोकसदाशान्ति तीनों शान्तियाँ सदा प्रदान करते हैं। जब युद्ध शान्त हो, जब मन शान्त हो, जब प्रकृति शान्त हो -- सब सर्वलोकसदाशान्ति का कार्य। सहस्रनाम के अन्तिम 40 में शान्ति-नाम -- यात्रा शान्ति की ओर बढ़ रही है।
modern_context_en: 'Om Shantih Shantih Shantih' -- triple peace: adhyatmik, adhibhautik, adhidaivik. Sarvalokasadashanti provides all three ALWAYS. When wars cease, when minds calm, when nature settles -- Sarvalokasadashanti is at work. A shanti-name in the final 40 signals: the Sahasranama's journey is moving toward its peaceful conclusion.
nri_context: When the relentless pace of immigrant life abroad finally pauses -- a quiet evening, a peaceful weekend, a moment of stillness -- Sarvalokasadashanti's eternal peace has briefly become tangible.
usage_context: Chant during Shanti Mantra recitation, peace meditation, or when comprehensive calm is needed. Ideal during evening prayers and when triple-shanti needs divine granting.

meaning_en: The supreme great Lord who commands the worship of every deity
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण देवताओं के महान प्रभु, सब सुरों के सर्वोच्च स्वामी
modern_context: प्रभु -- सर्वोच्च अधिकारी। सर्वदेवमहाप्रभु सब देवताओं के प्रभु -- शिव, ब्रह्मा, इन्द्र सब उनके अधीन। जब समुद्र मंथन में देवता विष्णु से प्रार्थना करें, जब गजेन्द्र मोक्ष में देवता स्तुति करें -- सब अपने प्रभु से बिनती कर रहे। सर्वदेवमहाप्रभु का दरबार ब्रह्माण्ड का सबसे भव्य -- सब देवता उनके दरबारी।
modern_context_en: Prabhu: supreme authority. Sarvadevamahaprabhu lords over all deities: Shiva, Brahma, Indra -- all under him. When devas pray to Vishnu during Samudra Manthan, when they offer stuti at Gajendra Moksha -- all petition their Prabhu. Sarvadevamahaprabhu's court is the cosmos' grandest: every deity is a courtier. The most powerful beings in creation bow before their master.
nri_context: When feeling small before powerful institutions abroad, Sarvadevamahaprabhu provides ultimate perspective: even gods bow before this Lord. No earthly authority outranks the divine Prabhu.
usage_context: Chant during comprehensive Vishnu puja, when divine supremacy needs affirming, or during Brahmotsavam. Ideal during Saturday archana and when the Lord's cosmic authority inspires.

meaning_en: The eternal and unfailing friend of every world and every being
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों के सदा मित्र, शाश्वत और अटूट मैत्री वाले
modern_context: गीता में कृष्ण कहते हैं 'सुहृदं सर्वभूतानाम्' -- सब प्राणियों का मित्र। सर्वलोकसदामित्र उसी मैत्री का शाश्वत रूप -- सदा मित्र, कभी शत्रु नहीं। सांसारिक मित्रता अस्थायी -- दोस्त बदलते हैं, भरोसा टूटता है। भगवान की मैत्री शाश्वत -- कभी नहीं बदलती, कभी नहीं टूटती, कभी धोखा नहीं। सबसे विश्वसनीय मित्र -- भगवान।
modern_context_en: Krishna says 'suhridam sarvabhutanam' -- friend of all beings. Sarvalokasadamitra IS that friendship made eternal: always a friend, never an enemy. Worldly friendships are temporary -- friends change, trust breaks. God's friendship is eternal: never changes, never breaks, never betrays. The most reliable friend in existence -- God -- whose friendship has zero failure rate across eternity.
nri_context: When friendships abroad prove transactional or temporary, Sarvalokasadamitra's eternal friendship fills the gap. The Lord is the one friend who never relocates, never loses touch, never changes.
usage_context: Chant on Friendship Day, when divine friendship needs celebrating, or during Gita Chapter 5 study. Ideal during lonely moments and when human friendships disappoint.

meaning_en: He who eternally works for the welfare of every living creature
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों का सदा हित (भलाई) करने वाले, शाश्वत कल्याणकारी
modern_context: सदा-हित -- हमेशा भलाई। सर्वभूतसदाहित सब प्राणियों की भलाई सदा करते हैं -- मनुष्य, पशु, पक्षी, कीट, वनस्पति -- बिना भेद, बिना रुके। जब सूर्य प्रतिदिन उगे (सदा-हित), जब बारिश ऋतु पर आए (सदा-हित), जब पृथ्वी फसल दे (सदा-हित) -- भगवान सदा सबका हित कर रहे, चुपचाप, बिना credit लिये।
modern_context_en: Sada-hita: always doing good. Sarvabhutasadahita eternally works for every creature's welfare: humans, animals, birds, insects, plants -- without discrimination, without pause. Every daily sunrise (sada-hita), every seasonal rain (sada-hita), every harvest (sada-hita) -- God perpetually does good for all, silently, without seeking credit. The universe's most productive welfare worker never takes a day off.
nri_context: When the daily functioning of life abroad seems automatic -- air to breathe, food to eat, water to drink -- Sarvabhutasadahita's silent, perpetual welfare work is operating behind every basic need.
usage_context: Chant during gratitude prayers, community welfare events, or when the invisible divine welfare needs acknowledging. Ideal during 'Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah' recitation.

meaning_en: He who is eternally worthy of worship by every god in every age
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण देवताओं द्वारा सदा वन्दनीय, शाश्वत रूप से सब सुरों से पूज्य
modern_context: सदा-वन्द्य -- हमेशा पूजनीय -- कल भी, आज भी, कल भी। सर्वदेवसदावन्द्य भगवान -- देवता भी उन्हें सदा पूजते हैं। सत्ययुग में भी, कलियुग में भी -- भगवान की वन्दनीयता कभी कम नहीं होती। जब सहस्रनाम 967 नामों तक पहुँचा -- 33 नाम शेष -- भगवान उतने ही वन्दनीय जितने नाम 1 पर -- क्योंकि 'सदा' में कोई कमी नहीं आती।
modern_context_en: Sada-vandya: always worshippable. Yesterday, today, tomorrow. Sarvadevasadavandya: even gods worship him ALWAYS. In Satya Yuga and Kali Yuga -- God's worshippability never diminishes. At name 967 -- 33 remaining -- God is as worthy of worship as at name 1. 'Sada' admits no diminishment. The closer to the summit, the greater the reverence.
nri_context: Through decades of immigrant life abroad -- youth to middle age to senior years -- Sarvadevasadavandya remains equally worthy of worship at every life-stage. The relationship never ages.
usage_context: Chant during daily worship, when the eternality of devotion needs affirming, or during multi-generational family prayer. Ideal during lifelong devotion and when worship spans decades.

meaning_en: He who eternally ensures the safety and preservation of every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों का सदा क्षेम (सुरक्षा/रक्षा) करने वाले
modern_context: गीता -- 'योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्' -- जो नहीं है वह देता हूँ (योग), जो है उसकी रक्षा करता हूँ (क्षेम)। सर्वलोकसदाक्षेमकर उस क्षेम-वचन का सार्वभौमिक और शाश्वत रूप -- सब लोकों का, सदा, क्षेम। जब बच्चों की सुरक्षा हो, जब बचत सुरक्षित रहे, जब स्वास्थ्य बना रहे -- सदा-क्षेम हो रहा।
modern_context_en: The Gita: 'yogakshemam vahamyaham' -- I provide what is lacking (yoga) and protect what exists (kshema). Sarvalokasadakshemakara IS that kshema-promise universalised and eternised: all worlds' safety, always. When children are secure, savings protected, health maintained -- sada-kshema is operating. The Lord's preservation service runs perpetually for every world.
nri_context: The dual NRI anxiety -- acquiring success abroad (yoga) and protecting what has been built (kshema) -- is exactly what Sarvalokasadakshemakara manages eternally through divine promise.
usage_context: Chant during Gita Chapter 9 study, when preservation of achievements is sought, or during daily protection prayers. Ideal during financial security prayers and when yoga-kshema needs divine backing.

meaning_en: The eternal giver of happiness to every devoted soul without pause
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को सदा सुख देने वाले, बिना रुके हर भक्त को आनन्दित करने वाले
modern_context: सदा-सुख-द -- हमेशा सुख देने वाला। सर्वभक्तसदासुखद -- हर भक्त को हर क्षण सुख। यह नाम बताता है कि भक्ति में दुख स्थायी नहीं -- सुख भगवान का default gift है। जब पूजा के बाद शान्ति, जब भजन में आनन्द, जब दर्शन में रोमांच -- सदा-सुख मिल रहा। भगवान का सुख-देना कभी बन्द नहीं -- expiry नहीं, ration नहीं, limit नहीं।
modern_context_en: Sada-sukha-da: always gives happiness. Sarvabhaktasadasukhada gives every devotee happiness at every moment. This name declares: in bhakti, sorrow is temporary; happiness is God's default gift. Post-puja peace, bhajan joy, darshan goosebumps -- sada-sukha flowing constantly. God's happiness-giving never stops: no expiry, no rationing, no limit. The most generous happiness-provider in existence.
nri_context: When daily devotional practice abroad -- even brief morning puja -- consistently produces peace and contentment, Sarvabhaktasadasukhada's perpetual happiness-delivery is proven through personal experience.
usage_context: Chant during daily puja, when happiness seems unreliable, or when God's consistent joy-giving needs celebrating. Ideal during kirtan and when bhakti-happiness outlasts worldly happiness.

meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha whose divine form embodies every beauty in creation
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सुन्दरता की जीवन्त मूर्ति श्री वेंकटेश, सब सौन्दर्य के साक्षात् स्वरूप
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वसुन्दरमूर्ति -- 970वाँ नाम, 97% पूर्ण। तीस नाम शेष -- शिखर दिख रहा है। 'सर्वसुन्दरमूर्ति' -- सब सौन्दर्य की जीवन्त मूर्ति -- तिरुमला बालाजी का सबसे सटीक वर्णन। काले पत्थर पर सोने के आभूषण, करुणामय नेत्र, मन्द मुस्कान -- 970 नामों ने भगवान के 970 पहलू दिखाए, और भगवान अभी भी नए सौन्दर्य दिखा रहे।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarvasundaramurti -- the 970th name, 97% complete. Thirty names remain -- the summit is visible. 'Sarvasundaramurti': the living embodiment of all beauty. Gold on black stone, compassionate eyes, gentle smile -- 970 names have revealed 970 facets, yet God still shows new beauty. The divine form is inexhaustible in its aesthetics.
nri_context: For every NRI at name 970 -- Sri Venkatesha Sarvasundaramurti IS the beauty that has sustained you through this entire journey. Thirty names to the summit. The Lord's beauty waits at the top. Om Sri Venkatesha Sarvasundaramurtaye Namah.
usage_context: Chant as the milestone name at 97% completion, during deity alankaram meditation, and when the Lord's beauty nears its fullest revelation at 970 of 1000.

meaning_en: The eternal Lord and protector of every world without interruption
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों के सदा नाथ (शाश्वत स्वामी/रक्षक)
modern_context: सदा-नाथ -- शाश्वत स्वामी, जो कभी retire नहीं होते, कभी छुट्टी नहीं लेते, कभी बदलते नहीं। सर्वलोकसदानाथ सब लोकों के सदा नाथ -- पृथ्वी का भी, स्वर्ग का भी, पाताल का भी। जब कोई कहे 'अनाथ' (बिना नाथ) -- सदानाथ कहते हैं 'कोई अनाथ नहीं; मैं सदा नाथ हूँ।' सहस्रनाम के अन्तिम 30 में 'नाथ' -- यात्रा के अन्त में भी नाथ साथ।
modern_context_en: Sada-natha: eternal lord who never retires, never takes leave, never changes. Sarvalokasadanatha: permanent protector of every world. When someone feels 'anatha' (lordless), Sadanatha responds: 'no one is lordless; I am always natha.' A 'natha' name in the final 30 affirms: even at the journey's end, the Lord accompanies.
nri_context: Through every phase of immigrant life -- arrival, struggle, settlement, ageing -- Sarvalokasadanatha has been natha throughout and will remain natha beyond. The Lord's lordship outlasts every visa and every lifetime.
usage_context: Chant when feeling lordless, during sharanagati, or when the Lord's eternal protection needs affirming. Ideal during solitary prayer and when divine lordship needs feeling.

meaning_en: The supreme and boundless grace flowing toward every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों पर महान कृपा बरसाने वाले, अपार अनुग्रह के स्रोत
modern_context: महा-कृपा -- महान कृपा -- सामान्य कृपा से कहीं बड़ी, अपार, असीम। सर्वभक्तमहाकृपा हर भक्त पर महाकृपा बरसाते हैं। कृपा बिना कारण, बिना शर्त, बिना माँगे -- यही महाकृपा। जब अचानक जीवन बदले बिना किसी प्रयास के, जब impossible possible हो जाए -- महाकृपा बरसी। भगवान की कृपा का rain forecast हमेशा 'heavy rainfall' है।
modern_context_en: Maha-kripa: supreme grace -- far beyond ordinary grace, boundless, limitless. Sarvabhaktamahaakripa rains maha-kripa on every devotee. Grace without cause, without condition, without being asked -- that IS maha-kripa. When life changes suddenly without effort, when impossible becomes possible -- maha-kripa has rained. God's grace forecast is always 'heavy rainfall.' The divine weather station predicts constant blessings.
nri_context: When life abroad transforms unexpectedly for the better -- a breakthrough no one planned, a solution no one engineered -- Sarvabhaktamahaakripa's heavy rain of grace has drenched you without warning.
usage_context: Chant when grace arrives unexpectedly, during gratitude for unmerited blessings, or when divine grace needs its supremacy celebrated. Ideal during Gajendra Moksha recitation and when miracles need explaining.

meaning_en: The perpetual giver of liberation to every being in every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों को सदा मोक्ष देने वाले, शाश्वत मुक्तिदाता
modern_context: सदा-मोक्ष-द -- हमेशा मोक्ष देने वाला -- मोक्ष का offer कभी expire नहीं। सर्वलोकसदामोक्षद -- सब लोकों को, सदा -- मोक्ष उपलब्ध। न सत्ययुग में ही मोक्ष बन्द, न कलियुग में। गीता का अन्तिम वचन 'मोक्षयिष्यामि' -- मुक्त करूँगा -- शाश्वत, अपरिवर्तनीय, सदा valid। सहस्रनाम के अन्तिम 28 नामों में मोक्ष-नाम -- यात्रा अपने अन्तिम लक्ष्य के निकट।
modern_context_en: Sada-moksha-da: always gives liberation -- the moksha offer never expires. Sarvalokasadamokshada: liberation available to all worlds, always. Not closed in Satya Yuga, not in Kali Yuga. The Gita's final 'mokshayishyami' -- I shall liberate -- is eternal, unchangeable, always valid. A moksha-name in the final 28 signals: the journey approaches its ultimate destination.
nri_context: When the ultimate spiritual aspiration crystallises abroad -- beyond career, beyond wealth, beyond status -- Sarvalokasadamokshada assures: moksha's offer is always open, regardless of location or era.
usage_context: Chant during moksha meditation, Vaikuntha Ekadashi, or when the Sahasranama nears its liberating conclusion. Ideal during final-phase spiritual seeking.

meaning_en: He who eternally dwells within every devoted heart as permanent resident
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों में सदा निवास करने वाले, हर भक्त के हृदय के शाश्वत निवासी
modern_context: सदा-वास -- शाश्वत निवास -- कभी नहीं छोड़ते। सर्वभक्तसदावास हर भक्त के हृदय में स्थायी (permanent) निवासी -- कभी बेदख़ल नहीं होते, कभी relocate नहीं करते। तिरुमला में भगवान एक मंदिर में रहते हैं -- पर सर्वभक्तसदावास करोड़ों हृदयों में एक साथ। भगवान का favourite address -- भक्त का हृदय -- और उनका lease -- अनन्त।
modern_context_en: Sada-vasa: permanent residence -- never leaves. Sarvabhaktasadavasa is the permanent resident of every devotee's heart -- never evicted, never relocated. At Tirumala, God resides in one temple. But Sarvabhaktasadavasa resides in crores of hearts simultaneously. God's favourite address: the devotee's heart. His lease: eternity. No landlord can evict the divine tenant.
nri_context: When Tirumala is thousands of miles away, Sarvabhaktasadavasa's permanent heart-residence makes every NRI devotee's chest a temple. The Lord has never been absent from your heart -- not for a single moment.
usage_context: Chant during heart-centred meditation, when God's inner presence needs affirming, or when physical temples are inaccessible. Ideal during Anahata meditation and personal devotion.

meaning_en: The eternal light that perpetually illuminates every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों की सदा ज्योति, शाश्वत रूप से सब संसारों को प्रकाशित करने वाली अमर ज्योत
modern_context: सदा-ज्योति -- शाश्वत ज्योत -- कभी नहीं बुझती। अखण्ड ज्योति (eternal flame) भारत के अनेक मंदिरों में जलती है -- पर सर्वलोकसदाज्योति सबसे बड़ी अखण्ड ज्योत -- जो सृष्टि से पहले से जल रही है और प्रलय के बाद भी जलेगी। 'तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय' -- उस शाश्वत ज्योत की ओर ले चलो। 25 नाम शेष -- ज्योत मंज़िल पर चमक रही।
modern_context_en: Sada-jyoti: eternal flame that never extinguishes. Akhand jyotis burn in many Indian temples, but Sarvalokasadajyoti is the greatest akhand jyoti: burning before creation, burning after dissolution. 'Tamaso ma jyotirgamaya' -- lead to that eternal light. Twenty-five names remain -- the jyoti shines at the destination, guiding the final steps.
nri_context: Every akhand jyoti lit at home abroad -- whether for days or years -- connects to Sarvalokasadajyoti's eternal flame. The home lamp borrows from the cosmic flame that has never gone out.
usage_context: Chant during Diwali, akhand jyoti lighting, or when the eternal light needs invoking. Ideal during Kartik month and when 'tamaso ma jyotirgamaya' is the prayer.

meaning_en: The eternal grace flowing toward every creature without exception
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों पर सदा कृपा बरसाने वाले, हर जीव पर शाश्वत अनुग्रह
modern_context: सदा-कृपा -- शाश्वत कृपा -- सब प्राणियों पर, सदा, बिना भेद। सर्वभूतसदाकृपा -- मनुष्य पर भी, पशु पर भी, पक्षी पर भी -- समान कृपा, सदा। जब बारिश सबको भिगोये -- अमीर-गरीब, मनुष्य-पशु -- वह सदाकृपा का प्रतीक। भगवान की कृपा बारिश जैसी -- सबको, सदा, बिना पक्षपात। 24 नाम शेष -- कृपा-नाम यात्रा के अन्तिम चरण में।
modern_context_en: Sada-kripa: eternal grace for every creature, always, without discrimination. Sarvabhutasadakripa: on humans, animals, birds -- equal grace, always. When rain drenches everyone -- rich-poor, human-animal -- that symbolises sadakripa. God's grace IS like rain: for all, always, without favouritism. Twenty-four names remain. A kripa-name in the final stretch assures: grace accompanies every remaining step.
nri_context: When divine grace abroad reaches not just you but your pets, your garden, your neighbourhood -- Sarvabhutasadakripa's species-blind grace is visibly raining on every creature around you.
usage_context: Chant during jiva-daya practices, when grace for all creatures needs celebrating, or during universal compassion prayers. Ideal during World Animal Day and when grace is recognised as universal.

meaning_en: The eternal auspiciousness that blesses every world without ceasing
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों का सदा मंगल (शाश्वत शुभता), बिना रुके सब संसारों को आशीर्वादित करने वाले
modern_context: सदा-मंगल -- शाश्वत शुभता -- कभी अशुभ नहीं। सर्वलोकसदामंगल -- सब लोकों में, सदा, मंगल (शुभता)। जब सुप्रभातम् 'मंगलम्' से समाप्त हो, जब आरती 'मंगलम्' से, जब कथा 'मंगलम्' से -- सब सर्वलोकसदामंगल को सम्बोधित। 23 नाम शेष -- मंगल-नाम -- यात्रा शुभता से समापन की ओर।
modern_context_en: Sada-mangala: eternal auspiciousness that never turns inauspicious. Sarvalokasadamangala: all worlds, always, blessed. When Suprabhatam ends with 'Mangalam,' when aarti concludes with 'Mangalam,' when katha closes with 'Mangalam' -- all address Sarvalokasadamangala. Twenty-three names remain. A mangala-name ensures: the journey concludes under an auspicious canopy.
nri_context: When every event abroad -- morning puja, evening aarti, festival celebration -- concludes with 'Mangalam,' Sarvalokasadamangala's eternal auspiciousness seals each occasion with divine blessing.
usage_context: Chant during Mangala Aarti, at ceremony conclusions, or when eternal auspiciousness needs invoking. Ideal during 'Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnuh' recitation and when shubhata needs sealing.

meaning_en: He who eternally ensures the welfare and blessedness of every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों का सदा कल्याण (शुभता/भलाई) करने वाले
modern_context: तिरुमला में 'नित्य कल्याणोत्सवम्' -- प्रतिदिन भगवान का विवाह-उत्सव -- शाश्वत कल्याण। सर्वभक्तसदाकल्याण -- हर भक्त का, सदा, कल्याण -- भगवान 'नित्य कल्याण' करते हैं। 22 नाम शेष -- कल्याण-नाम -- सहस्रनाम भक्तों के कल्याण की ओर अग्रसर।
modern_context_en: Tirumala's 'Nitya Kalyanotsavam': God's wedding celebrated daily -- eternal kalyana. Sarvabhaktasadakalyana: every devotee's welfare, always. God performs 'nitya kalyana.' Twenty-two names remain. A kalyana-name here signals: the Sahasranama advances toward its devotees' eternal welfare.
nri_context: When life abroad moves toward greater wellness -- health improving, family strengthening, spiritual deepening -- Sarvabhaktasadakalyana's perpetual welfare programme is visibly operating.
usage_context: Chant during Nitya Kalyanotsavam, when eternal welfare is sought, or during comprehensive blessing prayers. Ideal during wedding anniversaries and when God's perpetual care needs celebrating.

meaning_en: He who eternally creates fearlessness for every world and every being
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा अभय (निर्भयता) बनाने वाले, शाश्वत निर्भय-कर्ता
modern_context: बालाजी का उठा हुआ दाहिना हाथ -- अभय हस्तम् -- सदा उठा हुआ, कभी नीचे नहीं। सर्वलोकसदाभयकर उस शाश्वत अभय-मुद्रा का नाम -- सदा, सब लोकों में, अभय बना रहे। 21 नाम शेष -- अभय-नाम -- यात्रा के अन्तिम चरण में भगवान कहते हैं 'मत डरो, मैं सदा हूँ।' हाथ कभी नहीं झुकेगा।
modern_context_en: Balaji's raised right hand -- Abhaya Hastam -- is permanently raised, never lowered. Sarvalokasadabhayakara IS that eternal abhaya-mudra named: always, in every world, creating fearlessness. Twenty-one names remain. An abhaya-name here: God says 'fear not, I am always here.' The hand will never lower. The promise will never expire.
nri_context: Through every remaining name of this journey and every remaining day abroad, Sarvalokasadabhayakara's raised hand provides eternal assurance: fear nothing, for the Lord's abhaya is permanent.
usage_context: Chant during abhaya meditation, before frightening situations, or when the Lord's permanent abhaya needs invoking. Ideal during the Sahasranama's final phase and when courage needs eternal divine backing.

root_word: śrī + veṅkaṭeśa + sarva + loka + sadā + mokṣa + kara
meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha who eternally creates liberation for every soul in every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा मोक्ष बनाने वाले श्री वेंकटेश, शाश्वत मुक्तिकर्ता बालाजी
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वलोकसदामोक्षकर -- 980वाँ नाम, 98% पूर्ण। बीस नाम शेष -- अन्तिम दो प्रतिशत। यह नाम सहस्रनाम के सबसे समावेशी मोक्ष-नामों में है -- श्रीवेंकटेश (सगुण भगवान) + सर्वलोक (सब संसार) + सदा (शाश्वत) + मोक्षकर (मुक्ति बनाने वाला)। 980 नामों की यात्रा ने आपको मोक्ष के 20 कदम पास पहुँचा दिया। अब बस 20 नाम -- 20 कदम -- और सहस्रनाम पूर्ण। ॐ श्रीवेंकटेश सर्वलोक सदामोक्षकराय नमः।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarvaloka Sadamokshakara -- the 980th name, 98% complete. Twenty names remain -- the final two percent. This is among the Sahasranama's most comprehensive moksha-names: Sri Venkatesha (Saguna God) + Sarvaloka (every world) + Sada (always) + Mokshakara (liberation-creator). 980 names have brought you within 20 steps of completion. Just 20 names -- 20 steps -- and the Sahasranama is complete.
nri_context: For every NRI at name 980 -- you stand 20 names from completing the entire Venkateswara Sahasranama. Sri Venkatesha Sarvaloka Sadamokshakara has walked 980 steps with you and will walk the final 20. The summit is 20 steps away. The Lord who creates liberation across every world, always, awaits at step 1000. Om Sri Venkatesha Sarvaloka Sadamokshakaraya Namah.
usage_context: Chant as the penultimate milestone name at 98% completion, during moksha meditation, and when the Sahasranama's liberation-destination is 20 steps away. The definitive comprehensive moksha-name at 980 of 1000.

meaning_en: The eternal foundation supporting every devotee through every storm
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों का सदा आधार, हर तूफ़ान में हर भक्त का शाश्वत सहारा
modern_context: सदा-आधार -- शाश्वत सहारा। सर्वभक्तसदाधार हर भक्त का शाश्वत आधार -- जब जीवन हिले, आधार नहीं हिलता। 19 नाम शेष -- भगवान अन्तिम 19 कदमों में भी उतने ही दृढ़ आधार जितने पहले कदम पर। 'सदा' में कोई कमी नहीं -- पहला नाम और 981वाँ -- भगवान का सहारा समान।
modern_context_en: Sada-aadhara: eternal support. Sarvabhaktasadaadhara: every devotee's permanent foundation -- when life shakes, the foundation does not. Nineteen names remain. God is as firm a foundation at step 981 as at step 1. 'Sada' admits no weakening. The support is as strong approaching the summit as it was leaving base camp.
nri_context: Through every upheaval of immigrant life -- the entire Sahasranama journey -- Sarvabhaktasadaadhara has been the invisible foundation. Nineteen steps from the summit, the foundation is as solid as ever.
usage_context: Chant when stability is needed, during life's upheavals, or when divine support needs its permanence affirmed. Ideal during the journey's final phase.

meaning_en: The supreme lover who loves every world with boundless divine love
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों के महान प्रेमी, सब संसारों से अपार दिव्य प्रेम करने वाले
modern_context: महा-प्रेमी -- सबसे बड़ा प्रेमी। सर्वलोकमहाप्रेमी भगवान सब लोकों से सबसे गहरा प्रेम करते हैं -- माता-पिता का प्रेम बच्चों तक, मित्र का मित्र तक -- भगवान का प्रेम सम्पूर्ण ब्रह्माण्ड तक। कृष्ण ने गोपियों से प्रेम किया, राम ने सीता से, बालाजी पद्मावती से -- पर सर्वलोकमहाप्रेमी सबसे प्रेम करते हैं। 18 नाम शेष -- प्रेम-नाम -- यात्रा प्रेम से समाप्त होगी।
modern_context_en: Maha-premi: the greatest lover. Sarvalokamahaapremi loves every world with the deepest love. Parental love reaches children, friendship reaches friends -- God's love reaches the entire cosmos. Krishna loved gopis, Rama loved Sita, Balaji loves Padmavati -- but Sarvalokamahaapremi loves ALL. Eighteen names remain. A prema-name here signals: the Sahasranama will conclude in love.
nri_context: When the deepest human love abroad -- for family, for community, for the land you've made home -- feels profound, Sarvalokamahaapremi's cosmic love is the source from which every human love-drop is borrowed.
usage_context: Chant when divine love overwhelms, during Prema Bhakti meditation, or when the greatest love needs naming. Ideal during the Sahasranama's love-filled conclusion.

meaning_en: The eternal giver of liberation to every devoted soul who surrenders
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को सदा मोक्ष देने वाले, हर समर्पित आत्मा के शाश्वत मुक्तिदाता
modern_context: गीता का शाश्वत वचन -- 'मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः' -- मुक्त करूँगा, शोक मत कर। सर्वभक्तसदामोक्षद उसी वचन का शाश्वत रूप -- सब भक्तों को, सदा, मोक्ष। 17 नाम शेष -- सहस्रनाम के अन्तिम चरण में मोक्ष-नामों की बहुलता -- यात्रा का अन्तिम लक्ष्य स्पष्ट: मोक्ष।
modern_context_en: The Gita's eternal promise: 'mokshayishyami ma shuchah' -- I shall liberate, grieve not. Sarvabhaktasadamokshada IS that promise eternised: all devotees, always, moksha. Seventeen names remain. Moksha-names multiply in the final phase: the journey's ultimate goal is unmistakable -- liberation.
nri_context: As this Sahasranama approaches its summit, Sarvabhaktasadamokshada's promise accompanies every NRI devotee: the liberation that 983 names have been building toward is now just 17 steps away.
usage_context: Chant during moksha meditation, the Sahasranama's final stretch, or when liberation is the sole remaining aspiration. Ideal during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and deathbed prayers.

meaning_en: The eternal and complete auspiciousness pervading every world always
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा सम्पूर्ण मंगल (शुभता), शाश्वत सार्वभौमिक शुभत्व
modern_context: दोहरा 'सर्व' -- सर्वलोक + सर्वमंगल -- और 'सदा' -- शाश्वत। सर्वलोकसदासर्वमंगल -- सब लोकों में, सदा, सब शुभता। 16 नाम शेष -- मंगल-नाम -- हर पूजा 'मंगलम्' से समाप्त होती है -- सहस्रनाम भी मंगल की ओर बढ़ रहा।
modern_context_en: Double 'sarva': sarvaloka + sarvamangala -- plus 'sada' -- eternal. Sarvalokasadasarvamangala: all worlds, always, all auspiciousness. Sixteen names remain. Every puja ends with 'Mangalam.' The Sahasranama too advances toward its mangala-conclusion.
nri_context: As this sacred journey nears completion, Sarvalokasadasarvamangala seals every name chanted with eternal, comprehensive auspiciousness. Sixteen steps to the summit.
usage_context: Chant during ceremony conclusions, Mangala Aarti, or when the most comprehensive auspiciousness needs invoking. Ideal during the Sahasranama's auspicious final phase.

meaning_en: The supremely worthy refuge for every devotee who surrenders completely
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों के महान शरण्य (शरण लेने योग्य), सर्वोच्च समर्पण-पात्र
modern_context: महा-शरण्य -- सबसे अधिक शरण लेने योग्य। सर्वभक्तमहाशरण्य -- हर भक्त के लिए सर्वोच्च शरण-पात्र। शरणागति श्रीवैष्णव भक्ति का शिखर -- 'त्वमेव शरणं मम' -- तुम ही मेरी शरण। 15 नाम शेष -- शरणागति-नाम -- सहस्रनाम शरणागति की ओर समापन।
modern_context_en: Maha-sharanya: most worthy of refuge. Sarvabhaktamahasharanya: every devotee's supreme refuge-destination. Sharanagati is Sri Vaishnavism's pinnacle: 'tvameva sharanam mama.' Fifteen names remain. A sharanagati-name here: the Sahasranama moves toward its surrender-conclusion.
nri_context: As the Sahasranama approaches its final 15 names, Sarvabhaktamahasharanya invites complete surrender -- the ultimate act of devotion that every remaining name builds toward.
usage_context: Chant during sharanagati, when complete surrender is the prayer, or during the Sahasranama's final phase. Ideal during Dvadashi and when the Lord's worthiness of refuge needs ultimate affirming.

meaning_en: The eternal living form of bliss pervading and illuminating every world
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा विराजमान आनन्द की जीवन्त मूर्ति
modern_context: सदा-आनन्द-मूर्ति -- शाश्वत आनन्द की मूर्ति। तिरुमला 'आनन्दनिलयम्' -- आनन्द का निलय (निवास)। सर्वलोकसदानन्दमूर्ति उस आनन्दनिलय में विराजमान मूर्ति -- जो सब लोकों में, सदा, आनन्द बरसाती। 14 नाम शेष -- आनन्द-मूर्ति-नाम -- यात्रा आनन्द के शिखर की ओर।
modern_context_en: Sada-ananda-murti: the eternal form of bliss. Tirumala IS 'Anandanilayam': bliss's abode. Sarvalokasadanandamurti is the murti within that abode -- raining ananda across all worlds, always. Fourteen names remain. An ananda-murti name here: the journey climbs toward bliss's peak.
nri_context: With 14 names remaining, Sarvalokasadanandamurti assures every NRI devotee: the bliss you have experienced through this Sahasranama will not end at name 1000 -- it will become your permanent inner state.
usage_context: Chant during bliss meditation, Tirumala Anandanilayam contemplation, or as the Sahasranama approaches its blissful summit.

meaning_en: He who is eternally pleased and gracious toward every devotee
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों से सदा प्रसन्न, हर भक्त पर शाश्वत कृपा-दृष्टि रखने वाले
modern_context: सदा-प्रसन्न -- हमेशा प्रसन्न -- कभी क्रोधित नहीं, कभी असन्तुष्ट नहीं। सर्वभक्तसदाप्रसन्न हर भक्त से सदा प्रसन्न -- चाहे भक्त गलती करे, भूले, न पूजे -- भगवान सदा प्रसन्न। बालाजी की मूर्ति -- मन्द मुस्कान -- सदा प्रसन्न। 13 नाम शेष -- प्रसन्न-नाम -- भगवान आपकी यात्रा से प्रसन्न हैं।
modern_context_en: Sada-prasanna: always pleased -- never angry, never dissatisfied. Sarvabhaktasadaprasanna is always pleased with every devotee -- even when they err, forget, or skip puja. Balaji's murti: gentle smile -- always pleased. Thirteen names remain. A prasanna-name: God is pleased with your journey. Every name chanted has brought a divine smile.
nri_context: When guilt about imperfect devotion abroad weighs heavy, Sarvabhaktasadaprasanna's eternal smile dissolves it: God is not disappointed in you. He is sada-prasanna -- always pleased with you.
usage_context: Chant when seeking divine approval, when guilt needs dissolving, or when God's pleased disposition needs affirming. Ideal during personal devotion and when the Lord's smile needs visualising.

meaning_en: The eternally omniscient Lord who knows everything in every world always
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा सर्वज्ञ, शाश्वत रूप से सब कुछ जानने वाले
modern_context: सदा-सर्वज्ञ -- हमेशा सर्वज्ञ -- भगवान का ज्ञान कभी कम नहीं होता, कभी पुराना नहीं पड़ता, कभी update नहीं चाहिए। सर्वलोकसदासर्वज्ञ सब लोकों में, सदा, सब कुछ जानते हैं। 12 नाम शेष -- सर्वज्ञ-नाम -- भगवान जानते हैं कि आपकी यात्रा 12 कदम में पूर्ण होगी -- वे पहले से जानते थे।
modern_context_en: Sada-sarvajna: always omniscient. God's knowledge never decreases, never becomes outdated, never needs updating. Sarvalokasadasarvajna knows everything in every world, always. Twelve names remain. A sarvajna-name: God has known since before name 1 that your journey would reach name 988. He always knew you would make it this far.
nri_context: The Lord who knows everything already knew your entire immigrant journey before it began -- every challenge, every triumph, every prayer. Sarvalokasadasarvajna's omniscience includes your complete story.
usage_context: Chant when trusting divine omniscience, during decision-making, or when God's knowledge of your full story needs acknowledging. Ideal during the Sahasranama's omniscient conclusion.

meaning_en: The eternal and unfailing shelter for every devoted soul who seeks refuge
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों की सदा शरण, हर भक्त का शाश्वत और अटूट आश्रय
modern_context: शरणागति -- सम्पूर्ण समर्पण -- भक्ति का शिखर। सर्वभक्तसदाशरण -- भक्तों की सदा शरण -- अन्तिम 11 नामों में शरण-नाम। 'सदा' -- कभी बन्द नहीं, कभी भरा नहीं, कभी शर्त नहीं। सहस्रनाम शरणागति की ओर समापन करता है -- भक्त समर्पित, भगवान शरण -- यही सम्बन्ध का सार।
modern_context_en: Sharanagati: complete surrender -- bhakti's summit. Sarvabhaktasadasharana: devotees' eternal shelter. A sharana-name in the final 11: the Sahasranama concludes toward sharanagati. 'Sada': never closed, never full, never conditional. The Sahasranama's ultimate message: the devotee surrenders, God shelters -- THIS is the relationship's essence.
nri_context: With 11 names remaining, Sarvabhaktasadasharana's message to every NRI devotee is clear: the shelter that sustained you from name 1 to 989 will shelter you eternally. The Lord's sharana never expires.
usage_context: Chant during sharanagati, when eternal shelter is sought, or during the Sahasranama's final surrender phase. Ideal during the culminating devotional act.

meaning_en: Sri Venkatesha who is the absolute Brahman revealed through every thousand names
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सहस्रनाम से प्रकटित परब्रह्म श्री वेंकटेश, हज़ार नामों के पार का सर्वोच्च सत्य
modern_context: श्रीवेंकटेशसर्वसहस्रनामपरब्रह्म -- 990वाँ नाम, 99% पूर्ण। दस नाम शेष -- अन्तिम एक प्रतिशत। यह नाम सहस्रनाम को ही सम्बोधित करता है -- भगवान वह परब्रह्म हैं जो हज़ार नामों से प्रकट होते हैं। 990 नामों ने 990 रंग दिखाए -- और भगवान अभी भी अनन्त। परब्रह्म -- सर्वोच्च सत्य -- जो नामों से प्रकट होता है पर नामों से सीमित नहीं। 10 नाम शेष -- दस अन्तिम कदम -- और सहस्रनाम का शिखर।
modern_context_en: Sri Venkatesha Sarva Sahasranama Parabrahma -- the 990th name, 99% complete. TEN names remain -- the final one percent. This name addresses the Sahasranama itself: God is the Parabrahman revealed through a thousand names. 990 names have shown 990 colours, yet God remains infinite. Parabrahma: the supreme truth expressed through names yet unlimited by them. Ten steps to the summit. The most sacred countdown: 10, 9, 8... toward 1000.
nri_context: For every NRI at name 990 -- you are TEN names from completing the entire Venkateswara Sahasranama. Sri Venkatesha Sarva Sahasranama Parabrahma acknowledges your devotion with the highest title: the absolute Brahman revealed through this very journey you have taken. Ten steps remain. The Lord who IS Parabrahman waits at step 1000. Om Sri Venkatesha Sarva Sahasranama Parabrahmane Namah.
usage_context: Chant as the ultimate penultimate milestone at 99% completion, during the deepest meditation, and when the Sahasranama's Parabrahman-destination is ten steps away. The definitive name at 990 of 1000.

meaning_en: The eternally worthy refuge for every being in every world at every moment
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों के सब प्राणियों के लिए सदा शरण्य (शरण लेने योग्य), शाश्वत सार्वभौमिक आश्रय

śaraṇya.en: worthy of refuge, fit to be surrendered to

modern_context: अन्तिम दस -- दसवीं मंज़िल से गिनती -- 991वाँ नाम शरणागति से शुरू। सर्वलोकसदासर्वशरण्य -- सब लोकों के, सब प्राणियों के, सदा शरण लेने योग्य। शरणागति सहस्रनाम का मूल सन्देश -- 990 नामों ने भगवान के 990 गुण दिखाए, और सब गुणों का सार एक -- भगवान शरण हैं। अन्तिम 10 कदमों में पहला कदम: शरणागति।
modern_context_en: The final ten begins. The 991st name opens with sharanagati. Sarvalokasadasarvasharanya: worthy of refuge for every being, in every world, always. Sharanagati IS the Sahasranama's core message: 990 names revealed 990 qualities, and every quality converges on one truth -- God IS refuge. The first of the final ten steps: surrender. Nine steps remain to the summit.
nri_context: As this Sahasranama reaches its final ten names, Sarvalokasadasarvasharanya offers every NRI the first step of the conclusion: surrender everything -- career, worries, hopes, fears -- to the one eternal refuge.
usage_context: Chant as the opening name of the final ten, during sharanagati, and when the Sahasranama's ultimate teaching -- surrender -- begins its culmination.

root_word: sarva + bhakta + sadā + sarva + mokṣa + da
meaning_en: He who eternally gives every form of liberation to every devoted soul
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों को सदा सम्पूर्ण मोक्ष देने वाले, शाश्वत सर्वमुक्तिदाता
modern_context: दूसरा अन्तिम कदम -- मोक्ष। गीता का शाश्वत वचन 'मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः' -- मुक्त करूँगा, शोक मत कर। सर्वभक्तसदासर्वमोक्षद -- सब भक्तों को, सदा, सम्पूर्ण मोक्ष। शरणागति (991) के बाद मोक्ष (992) -- क्रम सही: पहले समर्पण, फिर मुक्ति। आठ कदम शेष।
modern_context_en: The second final step: moksha. The Gita's eternal promise: 'mokshayishyami ma shuchah.' Sarvabhaktasadasarvamokshada: all devotees, always, complete liberation. After sharanagati (991) comes moksha (992) -- the sequence is correct: first surrender, then liberation. Eight steps remain.
nri_context: After surrendering at name 991, liberation arrives at 992. For every NRI who has surrendered anxieties, ambitions, and attachments -- Sarvabhaktasadasarvamokshada's liberation is the divine response.
usage_context: Chant as the second of the final ten, during moksha meditation, and when surrender has been offered and liberation awaits receiving.

meaning_en: The eternal and complete bliss pervading every world without exception
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों में सदा सम्पूर्ण आनन्द, शाश्वत सार्वभौमिक परम सुख
modern_context: तीसरा अन्तिम कदम -- आनन्द। शरणागति (991) → मोक्ष (992) → आनन्द (993) -- समर्पण के बाद मुक्ति, मुक्ति के बाद शाश्वत आनन्द। सर्वलोकसदासर्वानन्द -- सब लोकों में, सदा, सम्पूर्ण आनन्द। तैत्तिरीय उपनिषद् -- 'आनन्दो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात्' -- आनन्द ही ब्रह्म है। सात कदम शेष।
modern_context_en: The third final step: ananda. Sharanagati (991) → Moksha (992) → Ananda (993): after surrender comes liberation; after liberation, eternal bliss. Sarvalokasadasarvananda: all worlds, always, complete bliss. The Taittiriya Upanishad: 'anando brahmeti vyajanat' -- Brahman IS bliss. Seven steps remain.
nri_context: The sequence surrender → liberation → bliss maps the entire spiritual journey abroad: surrender anxieties, receive freedom, experience permanent joy. Sarvalokasadasarvananda IS that final joy.
usage_context: Chant as the third of the final ten, during bliss meditation, and when the Sahasranama's ultimate promise -- eternal ananda -- needs feeling.

meaning_en: The eternal and complete auspiciousness blessing every devotee always
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण भक्तों का सदा सम्पूर्ण मंगल (शुभता), हर भक्त का शाश्वत आशीर्वाद
modern_context: चौथा अन्तिम कदम -- मंगल। हर पूजा 'मंगलम्' से समाप्त होती है -- सहस्रनाम भी मंगल की ओर बढ़ रहा। सर्वभक्तसदासर्वमंगल -- सब भक्तों पर, सदा, सम्पूर्ण शुभता। 'मंगलम् भगवान् विष्णुः मंगलम् गरुडध्वजः' -- इसी स्तुति का सार। छह कदम शेष।
modern_context_en: The fourth final step: mangala. Every puja ends with 'Mangalam' -- the Sahasranama too advances toward mangala. Sarvabhaktasadasarvamangala: all devotees, always, complete auspiciousness. 'Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnuh Mangalam Garudadhvajah' -- this stotra's essence. Six steps remain.
nri_context: As every ceremony abroad concludes with 'Mangalam,' this Sahasranama's conclusion seals every name chanted with Sarvabhaktasadasarvamangala's comprehensive, eternal auspiciousness.
usage_context: Chant as the fourth of the final ten, during Mangala Aarti, and when the Sahasranama's auspicious conclusion nears.

meaning_en: The eternal and complete welfare of every world and every being combined
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण लोकों का सदा सम्पूर्ण कल्याण, शाश्वत सार्वभौमिक भलाई
modern_context: पाँचवाँ अन्तिम कदम -- कल्याण। तिरुमला 'नित्य कल्याणोत्सवम्' -- प्रतिदिन कल्याण-उत्सव। सर्वलोकसदासर्वकल्याण -- सब लोकों का, सदा, सम्पूर्ण कल्याण -- भगवान का शाश्वत कल्याणकारी स्वभाव। पाँच कदम शेष -- आधा अन्तिम दस पूर्ण।
